ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Barren River) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the region */,
  "gdp" REAL /* Gross Domestic Product of the region */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Current unemployment rate in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wages" (
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wage record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region associated with the wage data */,
  "min_wage" REAL /* Minimum wage reported in the region */,
  "avg_wage" REAL /* Average wage reported in the region */,
  "max_wage" REAL /* Maximum wage reported in the region */,
  "num_employees" INTEGER /* Total number of employees represented in the wage data */,
  "counties_responding" INTEGER /* Count of counties that provided wage data */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector of the economy */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the wage data */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the wage data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wages_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "county_name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region the county belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the county */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Current unemployment rate in the county */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median income in the county */,
  "major_industries" TEXT /* Major industries in the county */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_counties_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "businesses" (
  "business_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each business */,
  "business_name" TEXT /* Name of the business */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county where the business is located */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the business */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the business */,
  "num_employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the business */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the business */,
  "establishment_date" TEXT /* Date the business was established */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("business_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_businesses_county_id FOREIGN KEY ("county_id") REFERENCES counties ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "business_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the business providing the response */,
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the wage record associated with the response */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the response was provided */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Any additional comments from the business */,
  "response_status" TEXT /* Status of the response */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_business_id FOREIGN KEY ("business_id") REFERENCES businesses ("business_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_wage_id FOREIGN KEY ("wage_id") REFERENCES wages ("wage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the data */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed */,
  "accessed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data included in the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
